Contents

    Corporate AI Training

    Team upskilling programs for organizations that want to build internal AI capability.

    Program Overview

    We come into existing corporate teams and upskill your employees. Focus is on integration—helping teams understand where and how AI fits into their workflows, not just learning tool features.

    What We Cover

    Strategic Integration

    • Where AI adds value (and where it doesn’t)
    • Workflow analysis: identifying automation opportunities
    • Change management: reducing resistance, increasing adoption
    • ROI measurement: tracking time saved, quality improvements

    Technical Skills

    • Prompting strategies (meta-level principles)
    • Tool selection and comparison
    • Workflow design (AI + human collaboration)
    • Quality control and iteration loops

    Security & Compliance

    • Data privacy considerations
    • Confidential information handling
    • Audit trails and documentation
    • Risk assessment for AI use cases

    Typical Program Structure

    Phase 1: Assessment (1-2 sessions)

    • Current state audit: How is AI used now?
    • Workflow analysis: Where are the opportunities?
    • Team readiness: What’s the learning curve?
    • Goals: What outcomes are you targeting?

    Phase 2: Training (4-8 sessions)

    • Group workshops: Core concepts and strategy
    • One-on-one coaching: Individual workflow integration
    • Hands-on practice: Real work applications
    • Feedback loops: Measure and iterate

    Phase 3: Systems (Ongoing)

    • Documentation: Build internal playbooks
    • Peer learning: Create internal knowledge sharing
    • Continuous improvement: Regular check-ins and updates

    Outcomes

    Immediate:

    • Teams understand AI’s strategic role
    • Clear workflows for common use cases
    • Reduced fear and increased confidence
    • Measurable productivity improvements

    Long-term:

    • Internal capability (less dependency on external training)
    • Adaptive approach (teams adjust as tools evolve)
    • Sustainable adoption (AI becomes part of culture, not a fad)
    • Competitive advantage (teams that understand AI outperform)

    Who This Is For

    Ideal clients:

    • Teams already using AI but hitting limits
    • Teams ready to invest in capability building
    • Organizations that value strategic thinking over quick fixes
    • Companies that want sustainable, not reactive, AI adoption

    Not ideal for:

    • Teams wanting “AI in 60 minutes” quick fixes
    • Organizations looking for one-size-fits-all training
    • Companies that want tools, not understanding

    Investment

    Training is customized to team size, goals, and timeline. Typical programs range from:

    • Pilot session: Single session audit and roadmap
    • Short program: 4-6 sessions for focused skill-building
    • Extended program: 8+ sessions for comprehensive capability building

    All programs include:

    • Pre-session preparation materials
    • Session recordings and documentation
    • Follow-up support (30 days post-program)
    • Internal playbook templates

    Next Steps

    Book a discovery call to discuss:

    • Your team’s current AI usage
    • Specific goals and challenges
    • Program scope and timeline
    • Investment and outcomes

    Questions? Reach out via email or LinkedIn.
